frame_analyses.description: First instruments visible in frame, graspers approaching the training setup with Penrose drain visible
Trainee working with suture and needle, appears to be threading through the Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument control, suture visible through drain

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot tying sequence, forming loops for first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be creating surgeon's knot with double throw

frame_analyses.description: Continuing first throw formation, instruments positioned to tighten knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good hand positioning, maintaining tension on suture

frame_analyses.description: Working on second throw, hands appear to have switched positions from first throw
Completing second throw, knot being tightened
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good control of suture tension, knot appears secure

frame_analyses.description: Final throw being performed, hands switched again
Beginning to cut suture ends with scissors
Cutting both suture ends close to the knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good cutting technique, appropriate length left

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot with double throw executed
Single throw with proper hand switching
Final single throw with hand switch completed

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain appears properly closed after knot tightening, no visible gaps

technique_summary: Excellent performance showing clear improvement from previous attempts. Proper surgeon's knot technique with appropriate hand switching between throws. Clean execution and good timing.
improvement_suggestions: Continue practicing to maintain this level of precision
Work on slightly faster needle loading to further improve time
strengths: Significant improvement in completion time compared to previous attempts
